text: The surgeon begins the thymectomy by dissecting between the adipose tissue and the pericardium, carefully following the course of the phrenic nerve upwards.
The surgeon uses fenestrated forceps and a bipolar Maryland dissector to retract and dissect along the tissue plane, demonstrating the wristed articulation of the instruments for precise orientation.

The surgeon uses wristed instruments to perform subtle sweeping motions, dissecting the fat between the innominate vein and rhema vein. The innominate vein, rhema vein, and superior vena cava (SVC) are clearly visible in the surgical field.
